meta.title: ideo

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: ideo
root.mentor_note: ideo is a small root carrying a large word: everything it builds is really about ideas.

attempt.direction_prompt: A single fact stated plainly, or a whole system of belief?

context_sentences: The two parties shared almost every policy but disagreed loudly over ideology.
His ideology had shifted so gradually over the decades that even he struggled to date the change.
Colleagues saw her less as a pragmatist and more as an ideologue who never once changed her position.
The new minister was no ideologue, and preferred whichever policy the evidence actually supported.
Long before any alphabet existed, early traders relied on a rough ideography of symbols carved into clay.
Traffic signs are a modern form of ideography, understood the same way in almost every country.

construct_options.text: The use of symbols to represent ideas directly, not sounds
The careful drafting of political speeches
The detailed mapping of a country’s geography

context_sentences: The workshop’s first hour was set aside just to ideate, with no idea yet allowed to be criticised.
She preferred to ideate on paper, sketching rough shapes long before any word was written down.

construct_options.text: To form an idea of something; to conceive or imagine
To carve a permanent inscription into stone
To rigidly follow a fixed system of belief
